key_points: Freshman dorms like Brooks are functional but visibly outdated
Hanselman rooms lack overhead lighting; bring a lamp
'Easy Street' dorms specifically called out as needing renovation
key_quotes: 'The dorms on Easy Street are in dire need of renovations'
'Looking exactly the same 14 years later'
'It is honestly ridiculous how poor quality these dorms are here...all while charging astronomical' tuition

key_points: Active party scene: dorms mid-week, bars Thu, house parties weekends
Darties hosted by sports teams are reportedly the highlight
Small school means tight social circles; some find it cliquey
key_quotes: 'Wednesday and Thursday tend to be bar nights, house parties or parties in on campus apartments Friday and Saturday'
'Darties are the most popular events'
'I graduated 14 years ago, but there wasn't a single weekend that went by that I didn't have multiple options'

key_points: Science Cafe mentioned positively for specific items like grilled cheese naan
No substantive complaints or praise about dining halls in data
Limited data makes a strong assessment impossible
key_quotes: 'What is the recipe for the grilled cheese naan? I used to get it in the science cafe all the time'

key_points: Some students report intense academic pressure and difficulty adjusting
Small campus means social struggles feel amplified and inescapable
Supportive environment mentioned by alumni but pressure is real
key_quotes: 'My college life was really depressive and overwhelming, especially as I came from outside and struggled to adapt as an introvert'
'At Holy Cross, I also had the hardest time of my life. But I think it shaped me'
'It's not a bad college. It's just that you have to have a tough skin'

key_points: Full rides available via QuestBridge; first-gen students report strong packages
Financial considerations reportedly influence waitlist outcomes
One student notes paying roughly $90k total out of pocket
key_quotes: 'JUST GOT A FULL RIDE TO COLLEGE OF HOLY CROSS'
'I believe it was bc of financials (and, therefore, I wouldn't get out the waitlist anyways)'
'i paid about $90,000 plus plane flights and my car'

key_points: Pre-med program is consistently praised; students feel well-supported
CS degree is theory-heavy; alumni warn it won't make you a job-ready developer
Small classes and accessible professors are frequently highlighted
key_quotes: 'Holy Cross pre-med is incredible. I went to Tufts and my sisters went to Holy Cross. They had a better pre-med experience'
'You will not become a developer if you cannot write code...CS at Holy Cross is of course full of coding courses, however there is a fundamental [gap]'
'Amazing faculty, small classes. I missed one class in four years'

key_points: Dining services imposed an English-only policy on multilingual staff, sparking student petition
Admissions office sends extremely detailed 45-page brochures as recruitment tactic
Waitlist management described as opaque; some feel finances drive decisions
key_quotes: 'dining services staff have been told to follow an English-only policy while working'
'I see where the tuition money goes lol' (re: 45-page brochure)
'We let in way too many people for our current freshman class, so it dropped from a 36% admit rate last cycle to 21%'

key_points: Campus is universally described as beautiful; a major selling point
Worcester, MA is not glamorous; students rely on shuttles or Ubers to get around
Weekend shuttles run to Target, Walmart, and cinemas but schedule is limited
key_quotes: 'Holy Cross is a great choice with a beautiful campus'
'A lot of kids Uber or have friends on campus with cars'
'Yes every weekend Saturdays and Sundays, there are shuttles leaving from the Hoval'

key_points: Alumni network is a frequently cited strength, especially in business and law
Pre-med feeder reputation is strong; healthcare advisory program mentioned
CS graduates may need to supplement degree with self-taught skills for tech jobs
key_quotes: 'Better alumni network' cited as key advantage over comparable schools
'Their Healthcare (and law) advisory programs are incredible'
'I was class of 2015 and they didn't teach any real world skills' (CS alum)

key_points: Full sticker price now ~$90k/year; several students call it hard to justify
Strong ROI if you get significant aid; poor ROI at full pay
One alum bluntly states it 'was not worth' $30k/year 25 years ago
key_quotes: 'Roughly $90,000 a year now for tuition and fees I believe? Total fucking joke. Not worth it'
'In the job market of 2026 there is borderline zero reason to spend this much money on school unless you genuinely want the name'
'i paid about $90,000 plus plane flights and my car'

key_points: Tight-knit Jesuit LAC with strong academics and a loyal alumni base
Not religiously oppressive but Catholic identity is present and visible
Works best for students who want small classes and a traditional college feel
key_quotes: 'Academically rigorous, close knit, high-quality education, great alumni network, excellent feeder school for graduate programs'
'Cons: Not the most diverse school'
'HC will change your life in the best possible way'

key_points: Cost: Full price ~$90k/year with alumni calling ROI questionable even at $30k
Administration: English-only policy for multilingual dining staff drew student backlash
Housing: Dorms described as unchanged in 14 years; some buildings specifically flagged as substandard
key_quotes: 'Total fucking joke. Not worth it. I went when it was roughly $30,000 a year 25 years ago and was also not worth that then'
'dining services staff have been told to follow an English-only policy'
'The dorms on Easy Street are in dire need of renovations'

key_points: Pre-med advising and healthcare program access is reportedly better than at larger schools
No mandatory major declaration early on; room to explore before committing
QuestBridge match makes it a genuinely free option for qualifying first-gen students
key_quotes: 'I had some time to explore around what I liked and ultimately I was able to find the right major for me'
'You get early access to courses so registration is easier' for pre-med
'Congrats! HC will change your life in the best possible way' — QuestBridge admit
